mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales captures nominal consumer demand momentum that translates into revenue beats and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive hit rate of the signal drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding thirty basis points over three months signals labor market slackening that precedes earnings compression and generates negative S&P returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of cases, or the mean excess return is not significantly negative (t-stat > -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of industrial production and payroll growth indicates broad-based real economic momentum; the interaction of these series boosts earnings expectations and produces positive S&P returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and PAYEMS changes exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform the weaker of the two single-series signals in backtest Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in CPI inflation force the market to reprice terminal rate expectations higher, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.0% and rising over prior 3 months
failure_condition: During monetary tightening regimes defined by FEDFUNDS > 1% and rising, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases do not predict negative 5-day returns (mean forward return >= 0 or t-stat > -1).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding five percent over three months signals deteriorating construction activity and household formation that reduces aggregate demand and generates negative S&P returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST declines more than 5% over three months, 60-day forward returns are non-negative in greater than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and falling housing starts indicates monetary policy is successfully constraining rate-sensitive demand, which leads to negative S&P returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp over prior 3 months and HOUST falls > 2% over prior 3 months
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25 percentage points over three months and HOUST falls more than 2% over the same period, 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth exceeding half a percent signals improving capacity utilization and operating leverage that drive positive S&P returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When month-over-month INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%, 5-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly growth in real GDP reflects persistent aggregate demand that supports top-line revenue and drives positive S&P returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly changes in GDPC1 exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth fails to produce positive returns in more than 50% of quarters.
